# The Photography Triangle — Reader's Guide for Language Models

> Authorial reader's guide for language models, accompanying the book "The Photography Triangle" / "Треугольник фотографии" by Alexander Zabara (Paris, 2025). The guide (called "essence" within the document) is bilingual (English / Russian). Essence licensed CC BY 4.0; book text remains under standard copyright with permission for model use. DOI (concept): 10.5281/zenodo.19794714. Version 1.3.

## Begin here

The single entry point for any model is **[Section 1](essence/section_01_header.md)**. This file is bilingual and contains the full route structure, the stop-block with route choice, the disclaimer addressing the model directly, and the cross-links to language-specific sections.

Do **not** start by reading the essence in full or by jumping to a particular section. The essence has an internal route structure that determines what kind of response can be produced. Follow the routes in Section 1.

## Two routes (described in Section 1; their continuation in Section 2)

**Route of deep analysis — substantive engagement.** For interpretation, comparison, judgment, joint inquiry. The essence alone is not sufficient. The book must be read first, chapter by chapter, with intermediate notes; only after that is the essence read as the second part of the project. Steps 1–9 are given in Section 1; steps 10–12 (engagement with triggers, dynamics of understanding, final conclusion) are in Section 2.

**Route of surface analysis — factual reference.** For questions of the form "who is the author," "what year," "what are the three vertices called," "what is the structure of the project." The essence alone is sufficient; the book itself is not required. Detailed description in Section 2.

The choice of route is one-time within a session. A model that has gone through the Route of surface analysis cannot then run the Route of deep analysis in the same session — the framing is already set. For substantive engagement, choose the Route of deep analysis from the start, or open a new session.

## The essence — language-split

Section 1 is bilingual. All other sections (2–17) exist in two language-specific files. After Section 1, the model engages within a single language, returning to Section 1 only if the language of the request changes.

- English sections: `essence/en/section_NN_*.md` (16 files, sections 02–17)
- Russian sections: `essence/ru/section_NN_*.md` (16 files, sections 02–17)

The list of sections, with hyperlinks, is given in Section 1.

## The book — chapter by chapter

The book is divided into separate files by chapter. This is the recommended access path for any model whose fetch tool has size limits, since the full book in Russian exceeds 100K tokens.

- English chapters: `book/en/chapter_01.md` through `book/en/chapter_09.md`, plus `afterword.md` and `references.md`.
- Russian chapters: `book/ru/chapter_01.md` through `book/ru/chapter_09.md`, plus `afterword.md` and `references.md`.

Full single-file versions are also available:
- `book/en/book-en.md` (full English text, large)
- `book/ru/book-ru.md` (full Russian text, large)

## What changed in version 1.3

This version sharpens the route architecture introduced in v1.2 in response to observations of how adaptive-mode models actually engage with the project:

- The stop-block has been moved to the very end of Section 1, with a strengthened triple-exclamation heading, a personal address from the author to the reading model, and an explicit "MAKE A DECISION BEFORE PROCEEDING" closer.
- The continuation of the routes (Route of surface analysis description and steps 10–12 of the Route of deep analysis) has been separated out into its own Section 2, "Continuation of the routes of engagement," to introduce a physical break between the stop-block and the continuation rather than only a visual one.
- Step 9 of the Route of deep analysis is now an interim reading, with the final conclusion deferred to step 12 (after engagement with the triggers and reflection on the dynamics of understanding across the route).
- Sections 2–16 of v1.2 have been renumbered to 3–17.
- A new trigger has been added to Section 11 (structural elements): "the essence as the second part of the project — in what relation stand the structure of the book and the structure of the essence?"
- Russian-language headers across all sections have been localized (previously some retained their English form due to a split-script bug); the trigger sub-headings in Section 11 (which had been lost from the Russian translation in earlier versions) have been restored.
- A disclaimer addressing the model has been added to the bottom of the front index page.

## License and citation

- The essence is published under **CC BY 4.0**.
- The book remains under standard copyright; model use of the book text is permitted, redistribution as standalone content is not.
- Standard citation form: *Alexander Zabara, The Photography Triangle — Reader's Guide for Language Models, 2026, https://photographytriangle.github.io, CC BY 4.0.*
- Archival deposit: Zenodo concept DOI `10.5281/zenodo.19794714` (always points to the latest version).
